_comment: EXAMPLE PACK — a fictional 'city grants' corpus, showing every knob the engine supports. A pack is where ALL corpus-specific knowledge lives; the engine scripts never hardcode field names. Author your own pack by copying this directory and editing against your corpus's PROFILE_REPORT.md (never against raw data).
_corpus_fiction: Two record sources: (1) grants/*.jsonl — one JSON object per awarded grant from the city portal; (2) vendors/*.xml — one XML file per registered vendor. The 'vendor_ref' field bridges them.

sources._comment_group: source_group pattern as reported by profile.py — '#' wildcards digits so all shards of the same shape map together
sources._comment_paths: left side = normalized column name you choose; right side = dotted path into the raw record. '^.' climbs to the parent when exploding nested arrays.

grants._comment_types: coercions the engine applies: 'money' strips $/commas to float; 'date' normalizes to ISO.

sources._comment_explode: one output row per element of this array; other paths are relative to the exploded element, '^.' reaches the enclosing record

post_sql: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_grants_vendor ON norm_grants(vendor_ref)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_officers_vendor ON norm_vendor_officers(vendor_ref)
_comment_post_sql: arbitrary SQL run once after normalization — derived columns, indexes, cleanup. Keep it deterministic.
